title: SELECT, Projection, Expressions, Types, and Result Contracts · Result-Grain Case: Alias Contracts
summary: An operations analyst needs a readable, reproducible ticket extract whose columns carry explicit meaning. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql alias contracts increment in a typed ticket projection query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use relation and column aliases to make provenance and output contracts unambiguous.
Compose SELECT and FROM clauses that project only required columns from a named relation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 5, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. A defensible solution applies Alias Contracts, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a typed ticket projection query pack.

content.code:
SELECT ticket_id,
       COALESCE(CAST(owner_id AS TEXT), 'unassigned') AS owner,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS severity_order
FROM tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NULL
ORDER BY severity_order, opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 7;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
